page.title: Secure File Encryption Tool for Email Attachments
page.intro: Sending a password-protected ZIP file and then emailing the password in a separate message is a practice known in Japan as PPAP (Password-protected ZIP → Password sent → Ango-ka / Encryption → Protocol). Today, many government agencies and companies have abandoned PPAP, recognising that it provides virtually no security benefit and may increase risk. This tool provides a simple but secure alternative for encrypting and decrypting files. It runs in your browser and never sends data externally. Highly recommended for those without file encryption software. This app is completely free—please use it freely and share it!
page.noscript: ⚠️ This tool requires JavaScript. Please enable JavaScript in your browser.
btn.description: 📖 Why password-protected ZIP emails are dangerous — and how to send attachments safely
lang.selector.label: 言語 / Language:
key.section.title: 1. Generate Key Pair
key.section.desc: Generate a public and private key pair
key.note.html: Note: The recipient generates the key pair on this page and sends the public key along with this page's URL to the sender. Never share your private key with anyone. The sender uses the received public key to encrypt the file. When the encrypted file is received, the recipient uses their private key on this page to decrypt it.
btn.downloadPublicKey: Download Public Key (to send to the sender)
encrypt.section.title: 2. Encrypt File (using Public Key)
encrypt.section.desc: Select a local file and encrypt it with the public key
label.publicKeySelect: Select a saved public key (or upload a file below):
decrypt.section.title: 3. Decrypt File (using Private Key)
decrypt.section.desc: Select a .encrypted file and decrypt it with your private key
label.privateKeySelect: Select a saved private key (or upload a file below):
info.title: How to Use
info.desc: The recipient generates a key pair and sends only the public key to the sender. The sender encrypts the file with the public key, and the recipient decrypts it with their private key.
info.steps.title: Step-by-step Instructions
info.receiver.label: Recipient:
info.receiver.1: Click "Generate Key Pair" to generate an RSA 2048-bit key pair
info.receiver.3: Send the public key file to the sender
info.receiver.4: Click "Download Private Key" to download private_key.pem and store it safely
info.sender.label: Sender:
info.sender.1: Select the received public key file (public_key.pub) under "Select public key file"
info.sender.2: Select the file to encrypt under "Select file to encrypt"
info.sender.4: The encrypted file (filename.encrypted) will be downloaded automatically
info.sender.5: Send the encrypted file to the recipient
info.decrypt.label: Recipient (decryption):
info.decrypt.1: Select your stored private_key.pem under "Select private key file"
info.decrypt.2: Select the received encrypted file under "Select file to decrypt (.encrypted)"
info.decrypt.4: The decrypted file will be downloaded automatically
info.security.title: Security Notes
info.security.1: All processing runs in the browser — nothing is sent to any server
info.security.2: Never share your private key with anyone
info.security.3: Store your private key in a safe location
info.security.4: If using on multiple computers, transfer your private key securely
info.install.pwa: On Chrome and Edge you can install this app locally from the install icon in the address bar.
info.license.title: License
info.license.text: This project is distributed under the MIT License. See the LICENSE file in the repository for details.

error.selectBoth.decrypt: Please select both a private key and a .encrypted file
keyhistory.title: Key Pair History
keyhistory.warning.title: ⚠️ Warning:
keyhistory.warning.text: This history is stored in your browser's local storage. Keys may be deleted if you clear your browser cache, reset your browser, or perform a factory reset on your device. Please store important private keys in a separate secure location as well.
confirm.deleteKey: Are you sure you want to delete this key pair?
confirm.clearAllKeys: Are you sure you want to delete all key pair history?
